title: feat: economics operations app (ingest agent, dashboard, tinybird backend)
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Adds the economics operations app: an agent-driven ingest pipeline plus a read-only web dashboard for tracking real spend, credits, cash runway, and product usage in Pollen.

- Backing data lives in enter.pollinations.ai's Tinybird project (`pollinations_enter` / `pollinations_enter_staging`), replacing the old standalone "operations" workspace.
- `op_pollen` is computed directly from `generation_event` (no manual invoice reconciliation) — cross-checked against the old invoice-based totals within ~1% for most GPU vendors.
- GPU margin is attributed by vendor+month using real per-request provider data, since per-pod/per-model attribution isn't reliable (one host can serve several models at once).
- Ingest agent and web app both point at the prod Tinybird workspace; staging is kept in sync for validation.

gist.headline: The hive gets a ledger
gist.blurb: A new operations dashboard gathers the hive's numbers in one warm little place. An ingest agent does the collecting; Tinybird does the remembering.
gist.summary: Added an agent-driven economics operations app and read-only dashboard backed by the enter.pollinations.ai Tinybird projects, with op_pollen computed directly from generation_event and GPU margins grouped by vendor and month.
gist.impact: Operations contributors can inspect spend, credits, runway, product Pollen usage, and provider-level GPU margins from one validated dashboard instead of reconciling separate finance and operations workspaces.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Pollinations' internal economics system as a cozy garden ledger: Tinybird-like data streams flow from retro terminals into labeled jars for Pollen, cloud spend, runway, and GPU vendors. Polly, wearing a tiny accountant visor and holding a clipboard, checks blooming code-plants while a CRT monitor robot happily charts the incoming data; the scene communicates reliable automated accounting rather than a user-facing product launch.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blushes, light blue wings, and a tiny accountant visor, checking a clipboard beside a glowing lime green (#ecf874) retro dashboard where data streams fill jars labeled "POLLEN", "RUNWAY", and "GPU"; a friendly CRT monitor robot points excitedly at a vendor-by-month chart while code-plants bloom around the desk. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel mint-lavender-peach gradients, warm lighting, subtle CRT glow, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, nostalgic and emotionally warm.
